scenario: Kestrel Telco evaluates a Ruby Zeebe client and a Go Zeebe client from the Camunda Community Hub to integrate Camunda 8.8 with 14 microservices handling 9200 events per day. The procurement team needs to understand the official support stance before signing the rollout, because Camunda commercial support depends on whether the chosen library is a community extension or an officially supported client.
question: Which statement matches the documented support stance for Camunda Community Hub clients in the page intro?

options.text: Camunda Community Hub extensions ship under Camunda commercial support contracts, so the Ruby Zeebe client and Go Zeebe client are covered alongside the official TypeScript SDK in production
Camunda Community Hub extensions are jointly maintained by Camunda and partner vendors, so the Ruby Zeebe client and Go Zeebe client receive enterprise SLA response times from Camunda directly

Camunda Community Hub extensions are listed on the page only as historical references and have been removed from active distribution, so the Ruby Zeebe client and Go Zeebe client must be self-built from forks

Camunda Community Hub extensions are maintained by the community and are not part of the commercial Camunda product, so Camunda does not support them as part of its commercial services to enterprise customers

a.text: Incorrect. The page intro explicitly states that community extensions are not part of the commercial Camunda product, so they fall outside Camunda commercial support contracts.

b.text: Incorrect. The page describes the libraries as community-maintained, not jointly maintained under enterprise SLAs from Camunda for partner vendors.

c.text: Incorrect. The page still lists active community-maintained libraries for Zeebe, Tasklist, Operate and other categories; they have not been removed from distribution.

d.text: Correct. The page intro states that community extensions are maintained by the community, are not part of the commercial Camunda product, and Camunda does not support them as part of its commercial services to enterprise customers.

explanation: The community clients page intro states that Camunda Community Hub extensions are maintained by the community and are not part of the commercial Camunda product, and that Camunda does not support them as part of its commercial services. Officially supported clients are the TypeScript SDK and the Camunda Spring Boot Starter.
